sound-and-song.opentdb.31fdbe70463eee68.explanation: Canadian singer-songwriter Leonard Cohen wrote Hallelujah.
sound-and-song.opentdb.31fdbe70463eee68.details: Cohen released it in 1984, but later recordings by John Cale and Jeff Buckley helped turn it into a modern standard.

sound-and-song.opentdb.87b5bd0d26c3f595.explanation: Gene Autry did not make the first recording of White Christmas.
sound-and-song.opentdb.87b5bd0d26c3f595.details: Bing Crosby introduced Irving Berlin's song in the 1942 film Holiday Inn; Autry pioneered recordings of several other Christmas favorites.

sound-and-song.opentdb.b24ae37660325d52.explanation: Nelly's Hot in Herre tells listeners to “take off all your clothes.”
sound-and-song.opentdb.b24ae37660325d52.details: The repeated response forms the chorus of the 2002 hit, whose title deliberately spells “here” as “herre.”

sound-and-song.opentdb.fd9a16175e66f648.explanation: Muse released its debut album Showbiz in 1999.
sound-and-song.opentdb.fd9a16175e66f648.details: The album introduced the band's dramatic vocals and alternative-rock sound before later records expanded into progressive and electronic styles.

sound-and-song.opentdb.ac541d374efcebb6.details: The ironic nickname emerged early in his career despite Clapton's reputation for fast, technically accomplished blues guitar playing.

sound-and-song.opentdb.eed0cd1f1452fa9c.explanation: Nirvana's Nevermind features a swimming baby on its cover.
sound-and-song.opentdb.eed0cd1f1452fa9c.details: Released in 1991, the album's success—especially Smells Like Teen Spirit—brought grunge into the pop mainstream.

sound-and-song.opentdb.d4f1be31941da552.explanation: Moving Pictures is a Rush album, not a Pink Floyd studio album.
sound-and-song.opentdb.d4f1be31941da552.details: Pink Floyd's catalog includes The Dark Side of the Moon, Wish You Were Here, Animals, and The Wall.

sound-and-song.opentdb.524601420c5cbffa.explanation: Basket Case is by Green Day, not Men at Work.
sound-and-song.opentdb.524601420c5cbffa.details: Men at Work's best-known songs include Down Under, Who Can It Be Now?, and Overkill.

sound-and-song.opentdb.822dce7c73118c21.explanation: Shout is a song by Tears for Fears.
sound-and-song.opentdb.822dce7c73118c21.details: The 1984 single uses an anthemic chorus and became one of the British duo's biggest international hits.

sound-and-song.opentdb.f2de304d80e6a3b7.details: The 1988 album brought West Coast gangsta rap to national attention and included Ice Cube, Dr. Dre, Eazy-E, MC Ren, and DJ Yella.

sound-and-song.opentdb.f9285c7fdf757e3c.explanation: Billie Joe Armstrong is Green Day's lead singer and guitarist.
sound-and-song.opentdb.f9285c7fdf757e3c.details: He co-founded the band with bassist Mike Dirnt and writes much of its punk-rock material.

sound-and-song.opentdb.1336ccae4f178e30.explanation: Carl Douglas recorded the 1974 hit Kung Fu Fighting.
sound-and-song.opentdb.1336ccae4f178e30.details: The disco novelty song became a worldwide number-one single and is remembered for its instantly recognizable martial-arts chorus.

sound-and-song.opentdb.6e81abacd2f72862.explanation: The Connells recorded '74–'75 for the album Ring.
sound-and-song.opentdb.6e81abacd2f72862.details: The reflective 1993 song became especially successful in Europe, even though the North Carolina band remained less commercially prominent in the United States.

sound-and-song.opentdb.85cdaedf1bbfab40.explanation: The electronic label Monstercat was founded in Canada.
sound-and-song.opentdb.85cdaedf1bbfab40.details: It began in Waterloo, Ontario, and later established its headquarters in Vancouver, releasing music across many electronic genres.

sound-and-song.opentdb.c8143b016ba4b4a1.explanation: Bob Dylan reportedly introduced the Beatles to cannabis in August 1964.
sound-and-song.opentdb.c8143b016ba4b4a1.details: The meeting took place at New York's Delmonico Hotel during the Beatles' first major U.S. tour.

sound-and-song.opentdb.35a005944c857af4.canonicalAnswer: Gimme! Gimme! Gimme! (A Man After Midnight)
aliases: Gimmie! Gimmie! Gimme! (A Man After Midnight)
sound-and-song.opentdb.35a005944c857af4.explanation: Madonna's Hung Up prominently samples ABBA's Gimme! Gimme! Gimme! (A Man After Midnight).
sound-and-song.opentdb.35a005944c857af4.details: ABBA rarely grants sample permission, making the recognizable 1979 synthesizer riff a notable foundation for Madonna's 2005 single.

sound-and-song.opentdb.617f792c38c24b54.details: The sped-up remix and anime-style internet meme led many listeners to assume the song was Japanese, but it originated with Swedish group Caramell.

sound-and-song.opentdb.f05cedd0393b3848.explanation: De La Soul provides the guest rap on Gorillaz's Feel Good Inc.
sound-and-song.opentdb.f05cedd0393b3848.details: The 2005 collaboration won a Grammy and contrasts De La Soul's laughter and verse with the song's melancholy sung sections.

sound-and-song.opentdb.920bf17c4be51564.explanation: The phrase “teenage wasteland” appears in the Who's Baba O'Riley.
sound-and-song.opentdb.920bf17c4be51564.details: Listeners often mistake the lyric for the title; the actual title combines names associated with Meher Baba and composer Terry Riley.

sound-and-song.opentdb.5bb2b826ef2ca7f6.explanation: Say You Will opens Kanye West's 808s & Heartbreak.
sound-and-song.opentdb.5bb2b826ef2ca7f6.details: Its sparse electronic pulse and Auto-Tuned vocals establish the album's themes of grief, isolation, and emotional vulnerability.

sound-and-song.opentdb.d2f2031c6468c242.explanation: M83's Midnight City appears on Grand Theft Auto V's Non-Stop-Pop FM station.
sound-and-song.opentdb.d2f2031c6468c242.details: The 2011 synth-pop track is known for its bright keyboard hook and climactic saxophone solo.

sound-and-song.opentdb.59771013cae6f82d.explanation: Pink Floyd's first studio album was The Piper at the Gates of Dawn.
sound-and-song.opentdb.59771013cae6f82d.details: Released in 1967, it is the band's only album made under the primary creative leadership of Syd Barrett.

sound-and-song.opentdb.1d6bae9832ac71dd.explanation: Waterloo became ABBA's first major UK hit.
sound-and-song.opentdb.1d6bae9832ac71dd.details: The song won the 1974 Eurovision Song Contest and launched the Swedish group internationally.

sound-and-song.opentdb.588c3cd4897b6a82.explanation: The Velvet Underground debuted with The Velvet Underground & Nico.
sound-and-song.opentdb.588c3cd4897b6a82.details: Andy Warhol produced the 1967 album and designed its famous peelable banana cover; Nico sings on several tracks.

sound-and-song.opentdb.ffbc87ad70422f36.explanation: Tame Impala released its second album, Lonerism, in 2012.
sound-and-song.opentdb.ffbc87ad70422f36.details: Kevin Parker recorded the psychedelic album largely alone, expanding the project's sound with prominent synthesizers.

sound-and-song.opentdb.9a6ba85410f63f49.explanation: Hollywood Undead released Notes from the Underground in 2013.
sound-and-song.opentdb.9a6ba85410f63f49.details: It was the rap-rock group's third studio album and debuted at number two on the U.S. Billboard 200.

sound-and-song.opentdb.5e67c1a8f712ecff.explanation: Love Lockdown, Paranoid, and Heartless appear on 808s & Heartbreak.
sound-and-song.opentdb.5e67c1a8f712ecff.details: Kanye West's 2008 album replaced much of his usual rapping and sampling with singing, Auto-Tune, and electronic percussion.

sound-and-song.opentdb.3531a40c8a57ee46.details: The acclaimed 1997 album explores technology, alienation, and modern anxiety through ambitious alternative-rock production.

sound-and-song.opentdb.42707e54984a2138.explanation: The Jets had a 1986 hit with Crush on You.
sound-and-song.opentdb.42707e54984a2138.details: The Minneapolis family group, made up of Wolfgramm siblings, blended pop, R&B, and dance music.

sound-and-song.opentdb.e7c09d9204625073.explanation: John Lennon praised Electric Light Orchestra as “sons of the Beatles.”
sound-and-song.opentdb.e7c09d9204625073.details: Jeff Lynne's group extended Beatlesque melodies and studio experimentation with prominent orchestral arrangements.

sound-and-song.opentdb.36a9266712331260.explanation: Foo Fighters released Sonic Highways in 2014.
sound-and-song.opentdb.36a9266712331260.details: Each song was recorded in a different American city, with an HBO documentary tracing the local musical history behind the sessions.

sound-and-song.opentdb.15f85c49ed71c83e.details: Nerdcore centers rap lyrics on subjects such as computing, gaming, science fiction, and other traditionally geek-associated interests.

sound-and-song.opentdb.94d3c736af165334.explanation: The quoted “I forgot my name” lyric appears in Eminem's Rain Man.
sound-and-song.opentdb.94d3c736af165334.details: Rain Man is a deliberately absurd track from his 2004 album Encore, distinct from the famous My Name Is refrain.

sound-and-song.opentdb.e1d04cfb327a9014.explanation: Strawberry Fields Forever is not on Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band.
sound-and-song.opentdb.e1d04cfb327a9014.details: It was released with Penny Lane as a double A-side single, although both songs came from the same creative period as the album.

sound-and-song.opentdb.b84bf990b30721ac.explanation: Avicii's Levels samples Etta James singing on Something's Got a Hold on Me.
sound-and-song.opentdb.b84bf990b30721ac.details: The gospel-inflected vocal fragment supplies the euphoric break in the 2011 electronic hit.

sound-and-song.opentdb.d327b1faad8194cf.explanation: Future appears on Grammys from Drake's Views.
sound-and-song.opentdb.d327b1faad8194cf.details: The two rappers had already collaborated extensively, including on their 2015 joint project What a Time to Be Alive.

sound-and-song.opentdb.81d615c576928e78.explanation: Dutch progressive-rock band Focus had the 1973 hit Hocus Pocus.
sound-and-song.opentdb.81d615c576928e78.details: The track alternates a heavy guitar riff with Thijs van Leer's yodeling, whistling, flute, and vocal effects.

sound-and-song.opentdb.c246e460cb5fbdf4.explanation: Perturbator released the synthwave album The Uncanny Valley.
sound-and-song.opentdb.c246e460cb5fbdf4.details: The 2016 record uses dark, cinematic electronic textures inspired by cyberpunk and 1980s horror and science-fiction soundtracks.

sound-and-song.opentdb.563a5b7fde875802.explanation: Trent Reznor founded Nine Inch Nails and remains its central creative force.
sound-and-song.opentdb.563a5b7fde875802.details: He writes, performs, and produces most studio material, while touring versions of the industrial-rock project use a full band.

sound-and-song.opentdb.9e3d88d6349858ce.explanation: R.E.M. released Out of Time in 1991.
sound-and-song.opentdb.9e3d88d6349858ce.details: The album includes Losing My Religion and helped transform the alternative-rock band into an international mainstream act.

sound-and-song.opentdb.7266cfbe3c9410ef.explanation: Michael Stipe was R.E.M.'s lead singer.
sound-and-song.opentdb.7266cfbe3c9410ef.details: His distinctive voice and often impressionistic lyrics fronted the band from its 1980 formation until its 2011 breakup.

sound-and-song.opentdb.63095922729f022f.explanation: George Gershwin composed Rhapsody in Blue.
sound-and-song.opentdb.63095922729f022f.details: The 1924 work blends classical concert music with jazz-influenced rhythm and harmony and opens with a famous clarinet glissando.

sound-and-song.opentdb.a924ab1b45fa7ec4.explanation: A caixa malacacheta is a type of snare drum.
sound-and-song.opentdb.a924ab1b45fa7ec4.details: The Brazilian percussion instrument supplies a sharp, rattling pulse in samba and other parade or ensemble traditions.

sound-and-song.opentdb.7e3c328b09c3e2ed.explanation: The narrative of good kid, m.A.A.d city unfolds in Compton, California.
sound-and-song.opentdb.7e3c328b09c3e2ed.details: Kendrick Lamar frames the album as a short film about adolescence, peer pressure, violence, faith, and escape in his hometown.

sound-and-song.opentdb.a34d9c76a456f18d.explanation: The Japanese title of Macintosh Plus's vaporwave track translates as Lisa Frank 420 / Modern Computing.
sound-and-song.opentdb.a34d9c76a456f18d.details: The slowed sample-based track from Floral Shoppe became one of vaporwave's most recognizable recordings and visual touchstones.

sound-and-song.opentdb.3371822a7d86a3cc.explanation: Prince's best-known backing band was the Revolution.
sound-and-song.opentdb.3371822a7d86a3cc.details: The group appears prominently on Purple Rain and combined rock, funk, pop, and R&B during Prince's mid-1980s peak.

sound-and-song.opentdb.e27669a30f4aa37e.explanation: Huun-Huur-Tu released Altai Sayan Tandy-Uula in 2004.
sound-and-song.opentdb.e27669a30f4aa37e.details: The Tuvan ensemble is internationally known for khoomei throat singing, in which one vocalist produces a drone and audible overtones.

sound-and-song.opentdb.7ca999540673bfce.explanation: Joy Division's Unknown Pleasures uses a pulsar signal visualization on its cover.
sound-and-song.opentdb.7ca999540673bfce.details: The stacked lines represent radio pulses from CP 1919; designer Peter Saville adapted a scientific diagram into an iconic image.

sound-and-song.opentdb.6d9f1d2134e317d7.explanation: Burial is not an alias used by Aphex Twin.
sound-and-song.opentdb.6d9f1d2134e317d7.details: Burial is a separate British electronic musician; Richard D. James has recorded as AFX, Polygon Window, and several other names.

sound-and-song.opentdb.13303f6dd30eeada.explanation: Lucky Lady Feel So Good is not a Tatsuro Yamashita song.
sound-and-song.opentdb.13303f6dd30eeada.details: The track is associated with city-pop artist T-Square's Masayoshi Takanaka, while Yamashita is known for songs such as Ride on Time and Sparkle.
